steps.title: Sketch the pole axis (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a long vertical line for the pole — the lamp's main axis. Check that it's truly vertical by holding your stylus up and comparing before committing the stroke.

steps.title: Add the curving arm (pencil)
steps.description: At the top of the pole, sketch a curved arm branching out to one side: it rises a little, then sweeps out horizontally, then curves back down like a shepherd's crook. Mark a small point where the lamp head will hang.

steps.title: Sketch the lantern head (pencil)
steps.description: At the end of the curving arm, draw a small lantern shape — a rectangle or faceted hexagon with a domed or peaked cap on top. It should feel like it's hanging, so place it just below where the arm ends.

steps.title: Switch to ink and draw the pole
steps.description: Switch to ink. Draw the pole as a single continuous vertical stroke from the ground to where the arm begins. Let the pole taper very slightly from base to top — thicker at the base is more stable.

steps.title: Ink the arm, lantern, and base (ink)
steps.description: Ink the curving arm in one smooth stroke. Ink the lantern head — sides, cap, and a small decorative finial at the top if you like. At the base of the pole, add a slight flare or an ornamental collar to show where the pole is anchored to the sidewalk.
